mission.title: vault: AWS backend stsAssume with "External ID"
mission.description: AWS backend stsAssume with "External ID". Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current vault setup
steps.description:
Verify your vault version and configuration:
```bash
vault version
```
This feature requires a working vault installation.

steps.title: Review vault configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant vault configuration:
**Feature Request:**
Security improvement for AWS backend "External ID" parameter for stsAssume operation.

steps.title: Apply the fix for AWS backend stsAssume with "External ID"
steps.description:
Vault added ExternalID support for AWS Auth STS configuration in PR #26628 (merged May 2024). This allows specifying an external_id when configuring AWS STS assume role operations, addressing the Confused Deputy security concern.
```bash
# Configure AWS auth with external_id for STS assume role
vault write auth/aws/config/client \
  sts_endpoint="https://sts.amazonaws.com" \
  external_id="your-external-id"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "AWS backend stsAssume with "External ID"" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Vault added ExternalID support for AWS Auth STS configuration in PR #26628 (merged May 2024). This addresses the Confused Deputy security concern by allowing an external_id parameter when calling sts:AssumeRole, which is especially important when assuming roles in foreign AWS accounts.

prerequisites.description: A working vault installation or development environment.
